items.title: Pipeline state machine, checkpoint, and --next/--step flags
items.context: internal/pipeline/ with state machine and step registry. Checkpoint read/write (.muster/work/{slug}/checkpoint.json). cmd/in.go with --next and --step flags. Steps are no-op stubs. Checkpoint round-trip tests, state machine transition tests. Testable: muster in <slug> --next advances through stub steps. Use TestCode and MockRuntime to test the command as thoroughly as possible; produce a manual-testing.md document listing items that require manual verification. See docs/2026-03-24-interface-refactor-and-pipeline-decomposition-design.md.

items.title: Pipeline git steps: worktree, prepare, finish
items.context: Implement pipeline steps that are pure git/config operations. worktree: git worktree add, write branch to roadmap item, copy pre-existing plan. prepare: merge main, version bump, changelog, roadmap cleanup, commit. finish: squash merge (direct) or create PR/MR (github-pr/gitlab-mr via VCS). Integration tests against real temp git repos. Use TestCode and MockRuntime to test the command as thoroughly as possible; produce a manual-testing.md document listing items that require manual verification. See docs/2026-03-24-interface-refactor-and-pipeline-decomposition-design.md.

items.title: Pipeline AI steps: plan, execute, review
items.context: Implement pipeline steps that invoke AI through CodingTool interface. plan: stage plan-feature templates, call CodingTool.Invoke, skip if plan exists. execute: stage execute-plan templates. review: stage review-implementation templates. Fully testable with TestCode: write expected responses, run pipeline, verify calls files. Use TestCode and MockRuntime to test the command as thoroughly as possible; produce a manual-testing.md document listing items that require manual verification. See docs/2026-03-24-interface-refactor-and-pipeline-decomposition-design.md.

items.title: Pipeline Docker steps: setup, verify, post-review-verify
items.context: Implement pipeline steps involving Docker through ContainerRuntime interface. setup: boot container via Up, run lifecycle.setup hook via Exec. verify: run lifecycle.verify via Exec, up to 3 AI fix attempts (combines ContainerRuntime and CodingTool). post-review-verify: verify again after review fixes. Testable with MockRuntime + TestCode together. Use TestCode and MockRuntime to test the command as thoroughly as possible; produce a manual-testing.md document listing items that require manual verification. See docs/2026-03-24-interface-refactor-and-pipeline-decomposition-design.md.

items.title: Pipeline batch mode: --all, --resume, --retry, locks
items.context: Orchestration on top of working pipeline. --all: parallel batch execution with claim locks (replaces roadmap-loop.sh). --resume/--retry: checkpoint-based recovery. internal/pipeline/lock.go: filesystem locks with stale PID detection (cross-platform). Lock acquisition/release/stale detection tests. Depends on pipeline-git-steps, pipeline-ai-steps, and pipeline-docker-steps all being complete. Use TestCode and MockRuntime to test the command as thoroughly as possible; produce a manual-testing.md document listing items that require manual verification. See docs/2026-03-24-interface-refactor-and-pipeline-decomposition-design.md.

items.title: Phase 7: init, doctor, help, and update commands
items.context: cmd/init.go generates .muster/ structure with --migrate for legacy files. cmd/doctor.go with tiered health checks (hard: git + claude/opencode, soft: docker, gh, glab), --fix, --config (resolved step config with source annotations), --models (provider/model validation). cmd/help.go with embedded reference docs and pipeline diagnostics. cmd/update.go with self-update via go-selfupdate. Deliverable: full CLI surface complete.

items.title: Phase 8: Polish and first release
items.context: Shell completion (bash, zsh, fish, powershell), install.sh for Mac/Linux, install.ps1 for Windows, README with usage examples, first tagged release v0.1.0, parity testing against existing shell scripts, manual verification on Windows with Docker Desktop and Git for Windows.
